calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.

APPEARANCES: Marissa Uchimura, Esq. of Maune Raichle Hartley French & Mudd, LLC 800/358-5922 for pltf & opposing party; Amber R. Craig, Esq. of DeHay Elliston LLP 510/285-0748 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Mathers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Dawn O'Neill and Penny Rushing and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The deposition testimony of George Kirk does not sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that the Kaiser Gypsum powder joint compound to which Mr. Mathers was exposed did not contain asbestos. Plaintiff's objections to the deposition testimony of George Kirk are overruled. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, the deposition testimony of Dawn O'Neill and Penny Rushing and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories would create a triable issue whether Mr. Mathers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
